elements.algorithm-2411-001: \par \fbox{ 0.969\textwidth{ Input: Accuracy parameter $\eps$; access to population expectations over $\calD$; norm upper bound $W$. \begin{enumerate} \item Let $h_0 = \bfzero$ be the zero function, and let $L_0 = \EE_\calD[y^2]$ be its loss. \item Run the GD algorithm from \cite{DiakonikolasKTZ22-agnostic-learning-unbiased-activations-GD} with accuracy $\eps$, and obtain ReLU neuron $h_{1}$ with loss $L_1$. This loss is competitive against the best positively-biased ReLU. \item Run the GD algorithm from \cite{AwasthiTV-ICLR23-ReLUGD} with accuracy $\eps$, and obtain ReLU neuron $h_2$ with loss $L_2$. This is competitive against the best moderately-biased ReLU. \item Run a grid search over parameter space $[0,W]\times [-O(\sqrt{\log(1/\eps)}), b_0]$ with accuracy $0.1\sqrt{\eps}$, where each pair of parameters $\{(\beta_i, \gamma_i):i\in 3,\ldots,N\}$ indicate our ``guesses" for $\|v\|$ and $b$. For each pair $(\beta_i, \gamma_i)$, we apply the following two subroutines on the normalized distribution $\hat \calD$ (\cref{prop:prelim:scaling-v}), and obtain ReLU $h_i$ with loss \[ L_{i} \leq 0.1\alpha\cdot \OPT_{i} + 0.1 \frac{\eps}{\beta_i^2}, \] where $\OPT_{i}$ is the minimum loss among ReLUs $\sigma(\lrangle{x,v}+b)$ with $\|v\| = \beta_i$ and $b = \gamma_i$. \begin{enumerate} \item Thresholded PCA (\cref{sec:filtered-PCA}): output $w_0$, the top eigenvector of the following matrix: \[ \EE_\calD\left[ x x^\top \cdot \indicator{|y| \geq \frac{1}{|b|}} \right]. \] \item Reweighted PGD (\cref{sec:reweighted-PGD}): an iterative GD-like algorithm with initialization $w_0$ and step size $\eta = c_\eta' /\Phi\big( \Theta(b)\big)$, for $O(\log(1/\eps))$ steps. On each iteration $t$, we update $w_t$ by: \[ w_{t+1} \gets \frac{w_t + \eta v_\mathrm{update}}{\|w_t + \eta v_\mathrm{update}\|},\text{ where } v_\mathrm{update} = \big(I_d - w_t w_t^\top\big) \EE\left[f_{t}(x)\cdot y\cdot \indicator{\lrangle{x,w_t} \geq \Tilde{b}} \right], \] where $f_t(x)$ is a weight function and $\Tilde{b}$ a threshold value, both specified during analysis. \end{enumerate} \end{enumerate} Output: $h_{\mathrm{ind}}$, where $\mathrm{ind} = \arg\min_i\{L_i: i\in [N]\}$. } } \par \caption{Complete algorithm, assuming population access to $\calD$.}\label{alg:full-alg}

elements.section-2411-004: Reweighted Projected Gradient Descent and Guarantees
elements.sent-2411-252: In this section, we show that given the warm start provided by thresholded PCA, our proposed weighted PGD algorithm can find a constant-factor approximation to the best-fit ReLU.
elements.sent-2411-253: The pseudocode of this algorithm is presented in Algorithm \ref{alg:reweighted-PGD .}
elements.algorithm-2411-002: \caption{Reweighted Projected Gradient Descent}\label{alg:reweighted-PGD} Input: $w_0\in \RR^d, b\in\RR^-, \eta \in (0, 1)$\; \For{$t = 1,2,\ldots, T = O(\log(1/\eps))$} { Get estimation $\hat v_\mathrm{update}$ of \begin{equation}(I-w_t w_t^\top)\EE\left[\exp\left(\rho|b|\lrangle{x,w_t} - \frac{\rho^2b^2}{2}\right) \cdot xy\cdot \mathbbm{1}\{\lrangle{x,w_t} \geq (\rho+\lambda-\lambda^2\rho)|b| \}\right]\end{equation} from $n = \poly(d, \frac{1}{\delta}, \frac{1}{\eps}, B)$ fresh samples\; $\Tilde w_t \gets (w_{t-1}, b_{t-1}) - \eta \cdot \hat v_\mathrm{update} $\; $w_t\gets \frac{\Tilde w_t}{\|\Tilde{w}_t\|}$\; } $\mathrm{ind} \gets \arg\min_{t\in [T]} \{L(w_t, b)\}$\; \Return $w_{\mathrm{ind}}$.
elements.sent-2411-254: Let $\calD$ be a joint distribution of $(x,y)$ over $\RR^d\times \RR$ such that the $x$-marginal is standard Gaussian.
elements.sent-2411-255: Let $\OPT$ be the loss of the best-fit ReLU $\sigma(\lrangle{x,v +b)$ with $\|v\|=1$.}
elements.sent-2411-256: Further suppose $|y| \leq B$, and that the initialization $w_0$ has $\lrangle{w_0, v \geq 0.9$.}
elements.sent-2411-257: If bias $b$ satisfies $b \leq b_0 < 0$ for some constant $b_0$, then reweighted PGD with $\rho = 0.5, \lambda = 0.9$, and $\eta = \frac{c_\eta' {\Phi((1-\rho)b)}$ for some constant $c_\eta'$ can with probability $1-\delta$ output a unit vector $w$ such that: \begin{equation}\sigma(\lrangle{x,w} + b) \leq \alpha\cdot \OPT + \eps,\end{equation} using $\poly(d, 1/\eps, 1/\delta, B)$ time and samples per iteration, and in $\log (1/\eps)$ iterations.}
elements.sent-2411-258: We hereby introduce some notations used in this section.
elements.sent-2411-259: We will focus heavily on the plane spanned by vectors $w_t$ and $v$.
elements.sent-2411-260: Every $x$ on this plane can be written as $x = x_w\cdot w_t + x_\perp\cdot v^\perp$, where $v^\perp \perp w_t$ is the component of $v$ that is orthogonal to $w_t$: \begin{equation}v^\perp = \frac{v - \lrangle{v,w_t}w_t}{\|v - \lrangle{v,w_t}w_t\|}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-261: In \cref{subsec:PGD-weighted-PGD , we explain Algorithm \ref{alg:reweighted-PGD in greater detail and introduce parameters $\rho,\lambda \in (0,1)$ to be determined later.}}
elements.sent-2411-262: Then, we break down the proof of \cref{thm:PGD into three intermediate results: in \cref{subsec:PGD-contribution-relu we lower-bound the contribution from the ReLU.}}
elements.sent-2411-263: In \cref{subsec:PGD-contribution-noise we upper bound the contribution from noise and determine the choice of $\rho,\lambda$.}
elements.sent-2411-264: Finally, in \cref{subsec:PGD-making-progress we account for the sampling error, and show that $\|w_t-v\|$ decreases exponentially on each iteration w.h.p.}

elements.sent-2411-284: A second observation concerns the fact that $\|w-v\|$ must be large when $L(w) > \alpha\cdot \OPT$:.
elements.sent-2411-285: Suppose $\alpha$ is large and $b$ sufficiently negative.
elements.sent-2411-286: For any unit vector $w$, if $L(w, b) > \alpha\cdot \OPT + \eps$, then: \begin{equation}\|w-v\| \geq \Omega \left( \frac{\alpha\cdot \OPT + \eps}{\Phi(b)} \right).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-288: By an elementary inequality we have $L(w, b) \leq 2\OPT + F(w,b)$, which implies $F(w) > 0.4\alpha\cdot \OPT + \eps$ for large $\alpha$.
elements.sent-2411-289: Meanwhile, $F(w)$ is upper bounded by $\|w-v\|$ by the following: \begin{equation}F(w, b) = \EE[(\sigma(\lrangle{x,w} + b) - \sigma(\lrangle{x,v} + b))^2] \leq \EE[(\lrangle{x,w} - \lrangle{x,v})^2 \indicator{\lrangle{x,w} + b \geq 0 or \lrangle{x,v} + b \geq 0}] \leq 2 \EE[\lrangle{x, w-v}^2 \indicator{\lrangle{x,w} \geq |b|}] = 2\int_{|b|}^\infty \int_{-\infty}^\infty \lrangle{x_w w + x_\perp v^\perp, w-v}^2 \,d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) = 2\int_{|b|}^\infty \int_{-\infty}^\infty \left( x_w \frac{\|w-v\|^2}{2} - x_\perp \frac{\|w-v\|\|w+v\|}{2} \right)^2 \,d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) \leq \int_{|b|}^\infty \int_{-\infty}^\infty \big( x_w^2 \|w-v\|^4 - x_\perp^2 \|w-v\|^2\big) \,d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) = \int_{|b|}^\infty \big(x_w^2 \|w-v\|^4 + \|w-v\|^2\big) \,d\Phi(x_w) \leq \big( 1.1\|w-v\|^4 b^2 + \|w-v\|^2 \big)\Phi(b),\end{equation} for sufficiently negative $b$.

elements.sent-2411-296: Using the coordinate system of $w_t$ and $v^\perp$, we can write the desired expression as: \begin{equation}\EE \big[ f_t(x) \cdot x_\perp \cdot \sigma(\lrangle{x,v} + b)\indicator{x_w \geq (\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho)|b|} \big] = \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{-\infty}^\infty x_\perp \cdot \sigma(\lrangle{x,v}+b) \, d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) \geq \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{0}^\infty x_\perp \cdot \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \cdot \sigma\Big(x_\perp - \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b| \Big) \, d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{\sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b|}^\infty x_\perp \Big(x_\perp - \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b| \Big) \,d\Phi(x_\perp) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \cdot \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}} \cdot \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big)\cdot \Phi \big( \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)b \big) = \Omega(\|w-v\|) \cdot \frac{\varphi \Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \cdot \varphi \big( \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)b \big)}{\sqrt{1-\lambda^2} \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)^2 b^2} .\end{equation}.
elements.formula-2411-050: \begin{equation}\EE \big[ f_t(x) \cdot x_\perp \cdot \sigma(\lrangle{x,v} + b)\indicator{x_w \geq (\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho)|b|} \big] = \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{-\infty}^\infty x_\perp \cdot \sigma(\lrangle{x,v}+b) \, d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) \geq \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{0}^\infty x_\perp \cdot \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \cdot \sigma\Big(x_\perp - \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b| \Big) \, d\Phi(x_\perp)\,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \int_{\sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b|}^\infty x_\perp \Big(x_\perp - \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho)|b| \Big) \,d\Phi(x_\perp) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\rho + \lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty f_t(x_w) \cdot \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}^2} \int_{(\lambda - \lambda^2\rho) |b|}^\infty \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \,d\Phi(x_w) = \sqrt{1 - \lrangle{w_t,v}} \cdot \Phi\Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big)\cdot \Phi \big( \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)b \big) = \Omega(\|w-v\|) \cdot \frac{\varphi \Big( \sqrt{1-\lambda^2}(1-\lambda\rho) b\Big) \cdot \varphi \big( \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)b \big)}{\sqrt{1-\lambda^2} \lambda(1-\lambda\rho)^2 b^2} .\end{equation}

elements.sent-2411-306: For all unit vector $u$ such that $u\perp w$, we have: \begin{equation}\lrangle{v_noise, u} = O(\sqrt{\OPT})\cdot \frac{e^{-\frac{b^2}{2} \big( -\rho^2 + \frac{1}{2}(\lambda + \rho \lambda^2 - \rho)^2 \big)}}{|b|^{1/2}}.\label{eq:PGD-noise-contribution}\end{equation}.
